question.text: In a mutual fund scheme, no single investor shall account for more than what percentage of the corpus?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** No single investor shall account for more than 25% of the corpus of the Scheme/Plan(s). **The Trap:** 10% is the prudential norm for investment in instruments of a single issuer. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This prevents undue concentration risk and protects other investors from large redemption shocks.
